description: Lock clinical blood banking to Fantus's 1937 Cook County hospital blood bank and its direct compatibility, laboratory, and refrigerated-storage dependencies.

checks.reason: The node remains anchored to Bernard Fantus establishing the first United States hospital blood bank at Cook County Hospital in 1937.
The scoped node includes typing, matching, and controlled transfusion, so blood-group compatibility is required.
Fantus's blood bank operated as a hospital laboratory, but the broad laboratory-diagnostic node is retained as an enabling clinical-lab context.
Stored donor blood depends on refrigerated preservation rather than direct donor-to-recipient transfusion alone.
